_status: COMPLETE — field shape extracted from validate-asset-record.js + live records (H0, 2026-06-25).
_discriminator: A record is a VIDEO record iff it carries segments OR best_use OR eligible_slots; otherwise it is an IMAGE record. The two variants enforce different grounding rules (see _semantic_rules).
_semantic_rules_validator_enforced: demonstrates[].claim MUST be an id present in the run's CLAIM-LIST.json (no floating/off-vocab claims).
Grounding gate — IMAGE: every demonstrates[] entry must carry non-empty 'evidence' (cited pixel evidence). VIDEO: every entry must carry non-empty 'motion_value' OR 'evidence', AND an 'at' timestamp.
A classified record (has shot_type or best_use) MUST carry a demonstrates[] array; relevance-bucket records need not.
VIDEO records MUST carry best_use (enum), loop_safe (boolean), needs_audio (boolean).

image_record_shape.relevance: string — relevance-bucket verdict (e.g. relevant | maybe | reject)
image_record_shape.shot_type: enum:SHOT_TYPE — images only; videos have no shot_type
image_record_shape.in_frame: string — what is physically in the frame

demonstrates.claim: string — MUST be an id in CLAIM-LIST.json
demonstrates.evidence: string — REQUIRED non-empty; cited pixel evidence grounding the claim

image_record_shape.selection_note: string — why this asset was kept/cut
eligible_sections: string — LP sections this image can slot into

video_record_shape.segments: array — EDL-style segment list (presence marks this a video record)

demonstrates.claim: string — MUST be an id in CLAIM-LIST.json
demonstrates.at: string — REQUIRED timestamp/range where the claim is proven (e.g. '0:01-0:05')
demonstrates.motion_value: string — REQUIRED (or evidence); what the MOTION proves that a still cannot

video_record_shape.selection_note: string — why this asset was kept/cut
eligible_slots: string — LP slots this video can fill
